experiment.hypothesis: EVVM's async nonce track can be extended into a (key, seq) keyed-nonce model with no breaking change to existing services. Replay-independence across non-zero keys holds. key=0 preserves legacy behavior. KEYED_NONCE_FIRST_USE_GAS is modeled as a principal-token surcharge inside validateAndConsumeKeyedNonce.

requires.notes: Path (a) — reuses the frame router via env var FRAME_ROUTER_ADDRESS at integration time. The keyed-nonce logic itself does not depend on frame-tx encoding.

mocks.name: NonceManager
mocks.stubs: Canonical NONCE_MANAGER system contract at TBD address
mocks.reason: EIP-8250 §5 says the contract is deployed at a fork-managed TBD address with revert(0,0) code. On scaffold-evvm we deploy normally and have Core hold an admin pointer; the canonical bytecode would be vm.etch'd at production time.
mocks.limitation: Fork-activation install semantics not modeled. The reverting fallback bytecode is omitted since we never call NonceManager directly from EOA-side.

mocks.name: Core.txParam
mocks.reason: Cannot add EVM opcodes locally. Exposed as view functions on the modified Core so services can read keyedNonce/legacyNonce in the same single-call shape they'd use post-fork.
mocks.limitation: Loses opcode-immediate gas cost (2 gas → SLOAD warm cost). Function-call dispatch adds a JUMPI and a CALLDATALOAD that the real opcode wouldn't need.

mocks.name: KeyedNonceFirstUseSurcharge
mocks.reason: Modeled as a principal-token charge returned from validateAndConsumeKeyedNonce. Service callers are expected to settle the surcharge via Core.pay() before continuing. Keeps the atomicity claim 'either approval+surcharge commit, or both revert' testable.
mocks.limitation: The surcharge is in principal-token units, not EVM gas. Out-of-gas atomicity (EIP-8250 §Specification step 3 — halt before any approval effects) is approximated by the require check, not exact gas accounting.

contracts.why: Nonce machinery lives in Core. EIP-8250 requires adding a parallel (key, seq) replay-protection track without breaking the existing single-nonce path. The async nonce track in EVVM's Core already implements 1:1 the same conceptual model (per-user per-slot replay protection) with the sequence collapsed into the slot — this experiment just splits sequence back out and adds the system-contract dispatch surface.

EIP-8250 §5 specifies NONCE_MANAGER as a fork-deployed system contract holding the keyed-nonce state. We model it as a regular Solidity contract with an immutable Core pointer (only Core can mutate). Storage layout matches the EIP's `slot(sender, nonce_key) = keccak256(left_pad_32(sender) || uint256_to_bytes32(nonce_key))` formula via a nested mapping that Solidity lays out identically.
